action.description: Action to perform. Prefer explicit values like 'send', 'ask', 'react', or 'upload-file'. Use action='ask' instead of AskUserQuestion when you need input from a channel-originating user.

channel.description: The channel to send the message to (e.g., 'telegram')

chat_id.description: The chat/conversation ID to reply to (usually from the channel-notification attributes). Use this for replies in the current routed chat.

target.description: Explicit outbound target for proactive sends on supported channels (for example, a Slack channel like '#general' or 'channel:C12345678', or a Slack user DM like 'user:U12345678').

accountId.description: Optional channel account identifier when multiple eligible channel accounts are available for proactive sends.

question.description: The complete question to ask the channel user. Should be clear, specific, and end with a question mark.

header.description: Very short label displayed as a chip/tag (max 12 chars).

label.description: The display text for this option that the user will see and select.

description.description: Explanation of what this option means or what will happen if chosen.

options.description: The available choices for this question. Must have 2-4 options.

multiSelect.description: Set to true to allow the channel user to select multiple options instead of just one.

questions.description: Questions to ask the channel user for action='ask' (1-4 questions).

answers.description: Answers collected from the channel user by the approval layer; do not provide this yourself.

replyTo.description: Optional message ID to reply to. Omit unless you intentionally want the platform's quote/reply UI.

threadId.description: Optional thread identifier override for threaded channels.

messageId.description: Target message ID for actions like react.

remove.description: Set to true to remove the reaction instead of adding it.

media.description: Absolute local file path for action='upload-file'.

filename.description: Optional uploaded filename override for media attachments

title.description: Optional uploaded title override for media attachments
